“Milan? They’re one of the most important clubs in the world,” he told AS in his native Spain.

“We have a duty to take them back into the Champions League: it’s an exciting challenge. I’m happy to close my career at Milan: I’ll have to earn my starting position every day, in every training session.

“Ancelotti? I hope Napoli can go back to winning with him, but it’ll be difficult to take the throne away from them if they keep investing.

“I decided to leave Napoli a year ago. PSG made me an offer, but Napoli rejected it: it would’ve been easy, in the summer of ‘medical certificates’, to force a transfer.

“Then I go back to Chelsea, and my agents will decide where to go. Then the battle begins again.

“At Chelsea there are lots of great players, so the chance was little.

“I had a long time without playing, I think I was a year and a little without playing. So I was happy to be able to play in the Premier League again.”

BAYERN LOOK TO WERNER?

Bayern Munich are entertaining the possibility of pursuing RB Leipzig striker Timo Werner, according to Bild.

With uncertainty looming over Robert Lewandowski's future, the Bundesliga champions are preparing for life without the Poland international and have apparently identified Werner as the ideal candidate to lead the line.

However, the asking price may well turn out to be a stumbling block as Leipzig will demand around £100m for the 22-year-old.

Manchester United are willing to pay Real Madrid’s asking price to sign Gareth Bale


Manchester United are working to sign Gareth Bale to play in a forward line that would feature last season’s signings Romelu Lukaku and Alexis Sanchez. The transfer fee is expected to exceed £85m, while matching Bale’s personal terms at Real Madrid would place the 28-year-old on a par with Old Trafford’s highest earners.

The European champions’ willingness to sell Bale has been an open secret for months. Madrid president Florentino Perez wants to rejuvenate a squad that has changed little while winning three consecutive Champions Leagues. Real are prepared to sell at least one of Bale, Karim Benzema and Cristiano Ronaldo this summer.

By Football Italia staff

Napoli are ready to sell Jorginho to Manchester City or Manchester United, but not for under the €60m asking price.

Manchester City aren’t the only ones interested, as Manchester United and Jose Mourinho are also looking towards the 26-year-old.

Chelsea had been contenders if Maurizio Sarri had taken over, but seem to have abandoned their negotiations for the Coach.

Barcelona unwilling to lower price on Rafinha: the situation

Barcelona does not appear willing to place a discount on Rafinha's option. On Monday there was a meeting between the player's agents and his parent club to discuss the matter of a discount to return to Inter. But Barcelona have denied the request, and will not lower the figure, given the number of matches played this season, and will not agree to another loan. Inter will not be allowed to renegotiate any of the purchase options that were agreed to in January (€35m plus €3m bonus), but can reorganize some of the details of the payments, however Inter have not requested such changes yet. Barcelona has not lowered their demands for the player, and seem unlikely to be convinced by Inter.

TRIO TO LEAVE ATLETICO?

Three players could leave Atletico Madrid this summer to make way for new arrivals, reports AS.

Defenders Sime Vrsaljko and Stefan Savic and forward Kevin Gameiro have all received offers to leave the Wanda Metropolitano before the start of next season.

Croatia full-back Vrsaljko has attracted interest from Napoli, Juventus and Roma, while French hitman €20m-rated Gameiro is wanted by Valencia.

UNITED IN TALISCA BATTLE

Portuguese publication O Jogo claim Manchester United are locked in a battle with two unnamed Chinese Super League clubs for the signature of Anderson Talisca.

United have reportedly lodged a £35m offer to Benfica for the Brazilian, who has excelled during a two-year loan at Besiktas.

The Chinese clubs are understood to have matched United’s offer for the 24-year-old, who scored 20 goals in 47 games last season.

The League currently distributes all international broadcast revenue equally between the clubs.
